voices.output: Q1 layout shape, Q2 directory names, Q3 file names, Q4 entry-point coherence. Halt rule: if dominant axis is non-layout/non-naming, surface misroute instead of producing a target list.
In: tools/, bin/, root config (package.json, tsconfig.json, .gitignore, .oxlintrc.json), .github/agents/. Out: source code inside .ts files, test bodies, runs/*.json content.

MAP. Repo root: package.json name='symphony' (root pkg, line 2); bin/meta-score (sh wrapper, only bin entry, execs tools/meta-score/cli.ts); .github/agents/ has 8 files all named maestro-*.agent.md. tools/: cli.ts (EMPTY FILE), package.json name='@fluidx-tools/claude-tools' (DIFFERENT NAME FROM ROOT, references Microsoft ADO repo URL), ownership.json (Microsoft ADO area path 'OC\FluidX\LoopApp\Foundations' — FOREIGN ARTIFACT), just.config.cjs (Microsoft 'just-scripts' build tool — FOREIGN ARTIFACT), jest.config.js, tsconfig.json, tsconfig.typecheck.json. tools/meta-score/: cli.ts, meta-score.ts, prompts.ts, meta-score.test.ts. tools/symphony/: cli.ts, parse.ts, perform.ts, persistence.ts, legality.ts, types.ts; subdirs algorithms/{base,local}/, runs/, examples/. tools/symphony/algorithms/base/: feature.md, investigate.md, refactor.md. tools/symphony/algorithms/local/: investigate.md (only — local overlay only exists for investigate). tools/symphony/runs/: 3 investigation dirs. NAME-CONTENT MISMATCHES: (a) tools/cli.ts is empty — name implies entry point, content is nothing. (b) Repo is named 'symphony' AND there is tools/symphony/ — same name at two scopes. (c) The agents are all named 'maestro-*' yet live in a repo called 'symphony' and use code from tools/symphony/ — three names ('symphony' the repo, 'maestro' the agents, 'symphony' the runtime lib) for one product. (d) tools/package.json declares a different package name (@fluidx-tools/claude-tools) than the repo. (e) bin/ contains only meta-score (the legacy system) — no entry for the live maestro/symphony flow.

H-A vocab-leakage / triple-name (predict 4+ cites). The product has THREE names: 'symphony' (repo + tools/symphony/), 'maestro' (agents), 'meta-score' (legacy fallback + only bin entry). Cites: package.json:2, tools/symphony/, .github/agents/maestro*.md, bin/meta-score. H-B entry-point-fragmentation (predict 3+ cites). Three cli.ts files (tools/cli.ts EMPTY, tools/meta-score/cli.ts, tools/symphony/cli.ts); only bin/meta-score is wired; no canonical 'run maestro' command. Cites: tools/cli.ts, bin/, tools/meta-score/cli.ts, tools/symphony/cli.ts. H-C foreign-artifact-pollution (predict 3 cites). tools/ looks like it was lifted from another Microsoft repo — ownership.json with FluidX area path, just.config.cjs Microsoft build tool, tools/package.json's @fluidx-tools name. Cites: tools/ownership.json, tools/just.config.cjs, tools/package.json:2. H-D layout-flat-vs-nested (predict <2 cites). tools/{meta-score,symphony}/ structure is fine; algorithms/{base,local}/ overlay pattern is intentional. Likely NOT dominant.

verdict.reason: 4 hypotheses; H-A and H-B are leading

voices.output: PER-ITEM CLASSIFY. KEEP: tools/meta-score/ (name accurate), tools/symphony/{parse,perform,persistence,legality,types}.ts (load-bearing, well-named), tools/symphony/algorithms/{base,local}/ (overlay pattern is correct), tools/symphony/runs/ (name accurate). DELETE: tools/cli.ts (empty file, name lies). RENAME-OR-CLARIFY (vocabulary): the triple-name problem (symphony / maestro / meta-score) — needs a decision, not a mechanical rename. MOVE-OR-DELETE (foreign): tools/ownership.json (FluidX ADO metadata, irrelevant to public repo), tools/just.config.cjs (Microsoft just-scripts not used by any documented script — root package.json scripts are oxlint/oxfmt, not just), tools/package.json name field (@fluidx-tools/claude-tools doesn't match the public repo identity). UNRESOLVED: should there be a bin/ entry for the live maestro flow, or is maestro inherently an agent-mode (no CLI) and bin/ should only exist for meta-score? UNRESOLVED: is tools/symphony/algorithms/local/ supposed to grow (only one file today) or stay as one repo-overlay? Probably stay — the overlay pattern is documented in repo memory.

verdict.reason: 1 delete, 3 foreign-artifact moves, 1 vocab decision, 2 unresolved

voices.output: DOMINANT AXIS: vocabulary / naming (H-A) — three names for one product is the load-bearing newcomer pain. Secondary: foreign-artifact pollution (H-C) — tools/ownership.json + tools/package.json's name field + just.config.cjs read as 'this folder belongs to a different project', which compounds the vocab confusion because it suggests tools/symphony/ might be a port of something else. Tertiary: entry-point fragmentation (H-B) — empty tools/cli.ts is a small but real lie; bin/ asymmetry (only meta-score has a binary) is real but the live flow is agent-mode-driven, so it may not need a binary. NOT DOMINANT: flat-vs-nested layout (H-D) is fine. HALT CHECK: dominant axis is naming/vocabulary AND secondary is foreign-artifact (a pollution / move concern) — both fall inside the layout/naming template. NOT halting. The vocab item (#1 below) is largest pain but it's a DECIDE, not a refactor. Smaller items are refactor-shaped.

verdict.reason: Dominant axis: vocab/naming; secondary: foreign artifacts; no halt

voices.output: TARGET LIST (capped at 8, ranked). #1 [decide] DECIDE the product's canonical name — pick ONE of {symphony, maestro, meta-score} as the umbrella and describe the others as parts. Three candidate framings: (a) 'Symphony' is the pattern, 'maestro' is the agent that runs it, 'meta-score' is the legacy fallback (status quo, document it); (b) rename tools/symphony/ to something non-colliding (tools/runtime/, tools/score/) so the repo name 'symphony' stops doubling; (c) rename the repo itself. Cites: package.json:2, tools/symphony/, .github/agents/maestro*.md, bin/meta-score. Tag: decide. Prereq: none. #2 [refactor:delete] Delete tools/cli.ts (empty file with a misleading name). Cite: tools/cli.ts. Tag: refactor. Prereq: none. #3 [refactor:document] Add a 1-paragraph 'three names, one product' section to root README.md cross-referenced from tools/README.md, naming the canonical relationship. SHIPS BEFORE #1 lands; documents status quo. Cite: README.md (root), tools/README.md. Tag: refactor (document). Prereq: none. #4 [decide] DECIDE foreign artifacts — keep tools/ownership.json + just.config.cjs + @fluidx-tools name (signals this folder is a port/fork from a Microsoft repo), or strip them (this is an OSS repo named symphony, not a Microsoft FluidX project). Cites: tools/ownership.json, tools/just.config.cjs, tools/package.json:2. Tag: decide. Prereq: none. #5 [refactor:rename] If decision #4 is 'strip', rename tools/package.json's name field to match the repo (e.g., '@symphony/tools'). Cite: tools/package.json:2. Tag: refactor. Prereq: #4. #6 [refactor:delete] If decision #4 is 'strip', delete tools/ownership.json and tools/just.config.cjs (no script invokes just-scripts; root scripts use oxlint/oxfmt). Cites: tools/ownership.json, tools/just.config.cjs, package.json:17-21. Tag: refactor. Prereq: #4. #7 [decide] Should there be a bin/ entry for the live maestro flow, or is bin/meta-score the only binary because maestro runs in agent-mode (no CLI)? Cite: bin/, .github/agents/maestro.agent.md. Tag: decide. Prereq: #1 (the binary's NAME depends on the umbrella choice). #8 [refactor:rename] If repo-name disambiguation (#1 option b) is chosen, rename tools/symphony/ → tools/<chosen-name>/ — touches 30+ imports, runs/*.json paths, .github/agents/maestro.agent.md references. SCOPED LARGE. Cite: every tools/symphony/* path. Tag: refactor. Prereq: #1. SHIP-IMMEDIATELY (no decision needed): #2, #3. DECISION-GATED: #1 → #7, #8; #4 → #5, #6.
